title: 10 best herbal capsules for motion sickness
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for motion sickness are a natural solution to alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness, such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs like ginger, peppermint, and lavender, which help to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation in the digestive system. For example, products like Ginger-Zen or Motion-Ease use ginger extract to target nausea and discomfort caused by travel, boating, or other forms of motion. By providing relief from these symptoms, herbal capsules can improve daily life for individuals who suffer from motion sickness, allowing them to enjoy activities without fear of discomfort or disruption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain bioactive compounds that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. The capsaicin present in ginger has a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which soothes the stomach and reduces nausea. Additionally, the gingerols and shogaols in ginger have been shown to inhibit the activity of serotonin receptors, which can contribute to motion sickness. By targeting these underlying causes, herbal ginger capsules provide quick relief from dizziness, vomiting, and stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Ginger's Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

5-Hydroxytryptamine (5-HT3) Receptor Blockade: Ginger has been shown to block the action of 5-HT3 receptors in the gut, which helps alleviate nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness by reducing the transmission of serotonin signals from the gut to the brain.

Vasodilation and Increased Blood Flow: Herbal ginger capsules can help increase blood flow and dilate blood vessels, which may help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness, by improving oxygenation of the body's tissues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of ginger contains compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The bark of the ginger plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and stomach upset, making it a popular choice for motion sickness relief.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 teaspoons of dried ginger root zingiber officinale powder into a small bowl.
Mix the ginger powder with 4 ounces of distilled water in a saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Combine the strained liquid with 6 capsules of gelatin and fill the capsules by hand within 30 minutes of preparation.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain peppermint oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to ease stomach discomfort and reduce nausea. When taken before travel or during a period of motion, the capsules work to soothe the digestive system and calm the body's reaction to the physical movements. This helps to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and queasiness, making it easier for individuals to enjoy their journey without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint's menthol content helps to stimulate digestive enzymes, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms, such as dizziness and headaches.

Gastrointestinal soothing effects: The natural oils in peppermint capsules calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of nausea, bloating, and discomfort often experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, making them a suitable candidate for motion sickness relief.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains menthol and other volatile oils that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and nausea.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried peppermint leaves mentha x piperita in airtight containers stored away from light.
Weigh out one part dried peppermint leaves and two parts gelatin capsule filler into a mixing bowl.
Blend the mixture well for three minutes until it becomes uniform and smooth consistency.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the blend mixture using a capsule filling machine or scoop spoon.
Store the filled capsules in airtight containers at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. By reducing feelings of queasiness and unease, maypop capsules can help to mitigate the effects of motion sickness, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable during travel, whether by car, boat, plane, or train.
remedy_properties: Antihistaminic activity: Maypop capsules contain compounds that exhibit antihistaminic properties, which can help alleviate histamine-related symptoms such as dizziness and nausea associated with motion sickness.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The extracts of maypop have been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties, which can help reduce feelings of stress and anxiety that often exacerbate motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Maypop capsules also contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate the inflammatory response triggered by motion sickness, reducing symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and dizziness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Fruits: The ripe fruits of the Maypop plant contain flavonoids that help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried passiflora incarnata flowers measuring 400mg per capsule and pure gelatin capsules of size zero.
Mix dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 100% vegetable-based filler to achieve uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal powder mixture using a small spoon or funnel.
Seal the filled capsules by twisting and pressing the ends together firmly for 5 seconds.
Store sealed capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with motion sickness because it has a natural calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and nausea. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier for the body to adjust to changing movements. Additionally, valerian root has been known to stimulate the brain's GABA receptors, which helps regulate balance and equilibrium, further reducing the discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian root has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as anxiety and stress.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing effects can help reduce muscle tension and spasms caused by motion sickness, promoting a sense of calm and reducing discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have a sedative effect, which helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain volatile oils that help to calm the digestive system and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 6 hours.
Grind 250 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground root with 500 grams of capsule filler and 50 grams of silicon dioxide as anti-caking agent in a large bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules #0 with the prepared herbal mixture weighing approximately 400-500 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with motion sickness because of its natural properties that soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms such as nausea and dizziness. The herbal remedy has a sedative effect on the stomach, calming any irritation caused by turbulent movements, and promoting relaxation in the body. As a result, chamomile capsules provide relief from the discomfort and distress associated with motion sickness, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and composed during travel or activities that trigger the condition.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: Chamomile's calming effects help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with motion sickness, making it easier for the body to recover from the physical symptoms.

Antihistamine-like action: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have been shown to possess antihistamine-like properties, which can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce the inflammation and discomfort caused by motion sickness in the digestive tract, head, and neck.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain a higher concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds have been traditionally used in herbal remedies to calm the stomach and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers and dry them properly using air circulation for 24 hours.
Measure out 20g of dried chamomile flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the ground chamomile powder with 100g of gelatin capsules in a bowl, filling each capsule by weight to achieve 500mg per serving.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage.
Dispense one capsule every hour as needed for motion sickness relief, starting 30 minutes before travel and continuing after arrival for maximum benefit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural blend of herbs that work in harmony to soothe the digestive system and calm the nervous system. The Dong quai plant, also known as Chinese angelica, has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to treat symptoms associated with motion sickness, such as dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. When taken in capsule form, its active compounds help to stabilize the body's internal environment, reducing the discomfort and disorientation caused by motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dong quai's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and discomfort in the stomach and digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds present in dong quai capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the inner lining of the stomach and exacerbate symptoms of motion sickness.

Gastroprotective property: Dong quai's gastroprotective property helps to soothe and protect the digestive tract from irritation and inflammation, reducing the risk of nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain caused by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness, by reducing inflammation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica sinensis roots and stems of high quality.
Grind the dried herbs into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one part ground angelica sinensis with three parts carrier powder such as potato starch.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500-750 milligrams of the herb mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and alkaloids, that have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to alleviate digestive issues. The capsules work by soothing the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and spasms caused by motion sickness. They also help to regulate blood flow and circulation, which can become disrupted during travel, further alleviating symptoms of dizziness and nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Improved blood flow: Ginkgo biloba may help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by increasing blood flow to the brain and other affected areas.
Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by motion sickness, which can contribute to its symptoms.
Neuroprotective effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which may help mitigate the damage caused by repeated exposure to motion stimuli.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of soothing compounds that help calm the stomach and alleviate nausea, making them ideal for treating motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair tree possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the stomach and intestines, providing relief from motion-induced discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves from a reputable supplier.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 50g of the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100ml of boiling water to create an extract.
Allow the mixture to steep for 30 minutes before straining it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a standardized dose of 120mg of the ginkgo biloba extract and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the calming properties of lavender, which has a natural ability to soothe the stomach and reduce symptoms of nausea. The capsules work by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, two common contributors to motion sickness. Additionally, the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender may also help to alleviate any underlying gastrointestinal issues that can exacerbate motion sickness, providing relief from dizziness and discomfort during travel or other activities.
remedy_properties: Lavandulol: A sesquiterpene compound found in lavender, which has been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-emetic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Linalool: Another essential oil constituent in lavender, which has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate motion sickness.
Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic acid present in lavender, which exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of essential oils, including linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Seeds: The seeds of the English lavender plant are rich in fatty acids, particularly linoleic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe digestive issues associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning for optimal essential oil content and dry them immediately using a food dehydrator on low heat for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes until uniform consistency is achieved.
Mix the powdered lavender with 50 grams of vitamin e oil in a glass jar, allowing the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 24 hours to infuse the oil with the herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract for capsule filling purposes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the lavender-vitamin e extract and seal the capsules using a capsule filler machine or manual technique to produce a total of 100 filled capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural compound called parthenolide, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Additionally, feverfew's ability to relax the smooth muscle in the stomach and intestines may also contribute to its effectiveness in reducing discomfort and improving digestion during travel or other activities that trigger motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's primary bioactive compound, parthenolide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and nausea.

Feverfew's flavonoids: The flavonoids present in feverfew capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect the body against oxidative stress caused by prolonged exposure to motion, reducing its negative effects on the digestive system and overall well-being.

Sesquiterpenes: Feverfew's sesquiterpenes have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antihistamine-like activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness, vertigo, and nausea by reducing inflammation and histamine release in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Flowers: Contain parthenolide, a bioactive compound that has been shown to have anticholinergic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves and flowers with a 4% curcumin content.
Grind the herb mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powdered herb with 6 tablespoons of vegetable glycerin and 1 tablespoon of water to create a liquid extract.
Transfer the liquid extract to capsules and fill each capsule with a 500mg dose taking 30 minutes before motion.
Store the prepared capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Papaya capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the enzyme papain, which breaks down proteins into smaller peptides that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, the antioxidants present in papaya help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness and lightheadedness. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system, papaya capsules provide natural relief from motion sickness, making travel by car, plane, or boat a more comfortable experience.
remedy_properties: Carotenoids: Papaya's carotenoid content, particularly beta-carotene and lutein, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Vitamin C: The high vitamin C content in papaya capsules can help to reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms by boosting the immune system and fighting off oxidative stress caused by travel-related factors.
Papain: Papain, an enzyme found in papaya, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate gastrointestinal upset, cramps, and other discomforts often experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Papaya roots have been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Papaya seeds contain compounds like capsicidin, which has been shown to have anti-emetic effects, making them effective in reducing symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest carica papaya leaves, stems, and roots according to the required dosage of 10 grams.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator for four hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix one tablespoon of the ground carica papaya powder with 10 grams of gelatin in a bowl for ten minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture, then seal them immediately to preserve their potency within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with motion sickness because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to help the body adapt to stressors such as changes in motion or environment. The herb contains bioactive compounds that stimulate the body's natural response to motion, reducing feelings of nausea and dizziness. Additionally, ginseng has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach, further alleviating symptoms of motion sickness. By supporting the body's natural balance, ginseng capsules provide relief from motion sickness, allowing individuals to enjoy travel and activities without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety responses, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation.

Antioxidant activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties may help protect the body from oxidative damage caused by prolonged periods of motion, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with motion sickness.

Vasodilatory effects: Ginseng has been found to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood circulation and oxygenation to the brain, helping to alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and lightheadedness often experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ginseng are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing nausea and vomiting.
Rhyzomes: Ginseng rhyzomes contain a unique combination of saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids that work together to mitigate the effects of motion sickness and promote calmness.
remedy_recipe: Measure 250mg of dried panax ginseng root powder into a small bowl.
Weigh out 10:1 ratio of filler material such as silicon dioxide to the herb.
Mix the panax ginseng powder and filler material together thoroughly in a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend and seal tightly for storage.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-nausea properties. The root of the licorice plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to alleviate digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting. When taken as a supplement, herbal licorice capsules can help to soothe an upset stomach and calm the nerves, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. By doing so, they provide natural relief from the discomfort and disorientation associated with travel-related illness.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in nausea and vomiting.
Flavonoids: Exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce discomfort associated with motion sickness.
Triterpenoid saponins: Has a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, helping to relieve nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain caused by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a good source of glycyrrhizin, an active compound that has anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, making it effective in alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: The barks contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to have anti-emetic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate yourself ensure potency.
Grind 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle take 10 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with 50 grams of excipients such as starch and silica in a clean glass bowl combine evenly.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filler machine or do it manually carefully.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 12 months ensuring potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural compound called eucalyptol, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system. The capsules also contain other herbal ingredients that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which can alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, dizziness, and fatigue. By promoting relaxation and soothing the stomach, Tasmanian blue gum capsules provide natural relief from motion sickness, making it easier for individuals to enjoy travel without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cineole: A compound found in Tasmanian Blue Gum, cineole has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Tannins: The tannins present in the plant have been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by motion sickness.

Essential Oils: The essential oils found in Tasmanian Blue Gum, including cineole and other terpenoids, have natural sedative properties that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and disorientation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of eucalyptol, the roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum plant have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness.

Barks: The barks of the plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-nausea effects, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules for motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for six hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for efficient extraction.
Mix one teaspoon of powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100ml of boiling water in a glass container for ten minutes of infusion time to create a concentrated tincture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled tincture mixture at a dosage of 250mg per capsule and store in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain extracts from Vitex agnus-castus, a herb that has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance. Research suggests that chaste tree can help regulate the body's response to motion and reduce feelings of nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe the stomach and digestive tract, further reducing discomfort associated with travel-related motion sickness. By taking chaste tree capsules before traveling, individuals can enjoy a smoother ride and reduced symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Chasteberry's ability to dilate blood vessels helps alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by increasing blood flow and reducing constriction in the hands and feet, which can become cold and sweaty during episodes.

Serotonin regulation: Chaste tree's influence on serotonin levels in the body may help stabilize mood and reduce anxiety, which are common triggers for motion sickness, allowing individuals to better cope with symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Chasteberry's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate nausea, dizziness, and stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chaste tree roots are used to make capsules for motion sickness due to their high content of essential oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: Chaste tree seeds are used in capsule form to treat motion sickness because they contain a compound called casticin, which has been shown to have anti-nausea effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vitex agnus-castus leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier or grow your own in well-drained soil.
Cleanse the harvested herbs by rinsing them gently with distilled water and air-drying for 24 hours.
Prepare the dried herbs by grinding them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure uniformity.
Mix 400mg of the ground vitex agnus-castus powder with 100mg of gelatin and 50mg of silica in a small bowl, then fill empty capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze for longer shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with motion sickness because of its natural adaptogenic properties, which enable it to reduce stress and anxiety triggered by motion. The capsules contain extracts from the Withania somnifera plant, also known as Ashwagandha, which has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to alleviate symptoms of nausea, dizziness, and vomiting associated with motion sickness. By calming the nervous system and reducing inflammation, ashwagandha capsules can effectively alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, allowing individuals to travel or engage in activities without discomfort.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps the body adapt to stress and anxiety caused by motion sickness, reducing symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate the gastrointestinal discomfort and inflammation associated with motion sickness.

GABA-enhancing properties: Ashwagandha contains compounds that increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which can help regulate nausea and vomiting responses triggered by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of bioactive compounds, including withanolides and alkaloids, which have been shown to effectively alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: The bark contains triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ashwagandha roots with a minimum of 3% withanolides from reputable sources for a total quantity of 250 grams.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Sift the powdered root through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles for 5 minutes.
Fill #0 capsules with a mixture of 500 milligrams of the sifted ashwagandha powder and 250 milligrams of gelatin for 20 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place for at least two weeks to allow the ingredients to mature before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain curcumin, a potent bioactive compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin has been shown to alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by reducing inflammation in the gut and brain. Additionally, turmeric's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat any underlying infections that may exacerbate motion sickness. By taking turmeric capsules before traveling or engaging in activities that trigger motion sickness, individuals can experience relief from symptoms and enjoy a smoother ride.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by blocking prostaglandins, hormones involved in inflammation and pain.

Curcuminoids: The combination of curcuminoids (including demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin) in turmeric capsules has been shown to possess anti-nausea properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by reducing the activity of the neurotransmitter serotonin, a key player in nausea and vomiting.

Volatiles: Turmeric contains volatile compounds like turmerone and atlantone, which have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to reduce the discomfort and pain associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, the Rhyzomes are the primary source of turmeric's medicinal properties and are effective against nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Roots: The Roots contain a higher concentration of bioactive compounds than other parts, making them ideal for extracting curcuminoids and other beneficial compounds to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots measuring one hundred grams of fresh or dried material.
Dry the turmeric roots in a warm, dry place at room temperature for seven days.
Grind the dried turmeric roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for five minutes.
Mix the turmeric powder with 500 milligrams of rice flour and 50 milliliters of water to create a dough.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one hundred milligrams of the turmeric-dough mixture and store in an airtight container for six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain the herb salvia officinalis, which has a natural calming effect on the digestive system. When taken before traveling by car, boat, plane, or other means, sage capsules can help to soothe and regulate stomach muscles, reducing symptoms of nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness. By stabilizing the digestive process, sage capsules can provide relief from discomfort and allow individuals to enjoy their journey without interruption.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like compounds: Sage contains antihistamine-like compounds that help to counteract the effects of histamine, a chemical released during motion sickness, which can cause symptoms like nausea and vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as nausea and abdominal discomfort.

Digestive soothing compounds: The compounds present in sage capsules, including rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, have been shown to soothe the digestive tract and relax the muscles of the stomach, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: With a high concentration of essential oils, rhyzomes possess antispasmodic and carminative effects, helping to ease stomach discomfort associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves from your garden or purchase from a reputable supplier weighing approximately 100 grams.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by spreading them on paper towels for at least 24 hours before grinding into a fine powder.
Combine the ground salvia officinalis leaves with 50 grams of gelatin in a blender and blend until well mixed and powdered.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture per capsule using a capsule filler machine or scoop and pour method.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or refrigerate for longer storage life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain a natural compound called thymol, which has been shown to have anti-nausea properties. When taken before traveling, the capsules can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as dizziness and vomiting. The herbal extract works by relaxing the stomach muscles, reducing nausea, and improving digestion, allowing individuals to travel with greater ease and comfort. As a result, thyme capsules have become a popular natural remedy for motion sickness sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Thyme's antispasmodic compounds, such as thymol, help relax muscle spasms and cramps in the stomach and digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body caused by motion sickness, which can contribute to symptoms such as dizziness and discomfort.

Gastroprotective properties: Thyme's gastroprotective compounds, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to protect the stomach lining from irritation and damage caused by motion sickness, reducing the risk of gastric ulcers and bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have been shown to possess anti-nausea and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a popular choice for alleviating motion sickness symptoms.

Leaves: Thyme leaves contain a high concentration of essential oils, including thymol and carvacrol, which are known for their natural calming effects on the stomach and can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a trusted source ensuring potency.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using shade or low-temperature drying for 3 days.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of ground thymus vulgaris powder with 250ml of distilled water to create a tincture solution taking 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the tincture solution and seal immediately readying for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca capsules helps with motion sickness because they contain Peruvian ginseng, which has been traditionally used to alleviate nausea and dizziness. The herb's natural adaptogenic properties help to stabilize the body's balance and reduce stress on the digestive system, allowing individuals to better cope with the sensory conflicts that trigger motion sickness. Additionally, maca's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help to soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort associated with queasiness. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing symptoms, maca capsules can provide relief from the distressing effects of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Maca helps the body adapt to physical and environmental stressors, including changes in motion, by regulating cortisol levels and promoting a sense of balance and stability.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Maca's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and stomach discomfort.

Vasodilatory Properties: Maca's ability to dilate blood vessels may help improve circulation and reduce blood pressure fluctuations that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms like dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, which helps to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by stabilizing the digestive system.
Rhyzomes: Contains bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Barks: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the stomach, reducing discomfort caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried lepidium meyenii powder from a reputable supplier in 250mg increments.
Mix the lepidium meyenii powder with 500mg of silica gel powder and 100mg of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 750mg of the mixed powder using an encapsulation machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.
Take two capsules 30 minutes prior to travel and as needed while experiencing motion sickness.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat motion sickness with herbal capsules is to choose a supplement containing ginger, peppermint, or passionflower. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting, while peppermint can ease stomach discomfort and calm digestive issues. Passionflower, on the other hand, helps reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of motion sickness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal capsules are vertigo, labyrinthitis, and Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (BPPV). These conditions cause dizziness, nausea, and balance issues. Herbal supplements like ginger, passionflower, and vestibular sedatives have been found to alleviate these symptoms by reducing inflammation, calming the nervous system, and improving blood flow to the inner ear.
